title: passiflora rugosa
intro: Passiflora rugosa, also known as the wild passionflower or red passionflower, is a perennial vine native to South America, characterized by its distinctive, intricate flowers and edible fruit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its calming and sedative properties, which are attributed to its alkaloids, particularly harmine and harmaline. The medicinal properties of Passiflora rugosa are believed to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and insomnia, alleviating menopausal symptoms, and lowering blood pressure. Herbal preparations of Passiflora rugosa can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
